experiment: #93 — is the K2 ternary cliff a capability threshold (agnostic to dense vs MoE)?
metric: GSM8K flexible-extract 5-shot; K2/K3 are pure PTQ (no distill); MoE rows are the FAITHFUL runs (fused experts ternarized, router FP)
verdict: REFUTED as a clean threshold. FP-capability is at best NECESSARY-not-sufficient: no model below FP~0.69 retains K2, but above that, K2-survival is family/training-dependent, not capability-determined. Clear counterexamples (see below). Downgrades the earlier '3-MoE-point' claim that resistance tracks capability; corroborates 'training dynamics impact PTQ robustness' (arXiv:2510.06213).
counterexamples: Falcon3-3B (FP 0.693) K2 0.360 SURVIVES vs Qwen3-1.7B (FP 0.700) K2 0.000 DIES — same capability, opposite outcome
Qwen3-30B (FP 0.893) K2 0.680 SURVIVES vs Qwen3-8B (FP 0.913) K2 0.023 DIES — higher capability dies
Qwen2.5-Coder-7B (FP 0.777) K2 0.503 SURVIVES vs Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct (FP 0.827) K2 0.147 mostly dies — same family, code-FT more robust
caveats: All FP<0.69 rows have K2/FP <= 0.06, so low-FP models uniformly collapse (a floor exists) — capability is necessary.
K2/FP retention at low FP is noisy (small denominator). K3 recovers nearly everywhere (fragility is a K2-only phenomenon).
MoE points are single-seed N=150; dense ladder N=150 (Paper H). Qwen3-8B K2 0.023 from N=300 clean_sweep.
Qwen2.5-14B excluded (only FP+K3 run; flex<strict extractor inversion flagged in source).
Attention-only confounded MoE runs (experts left FP) were EXCLUDED; only faithful expert-ternarized runs used.
